channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: ChatGPT’s latest feature is a transformative leap for web research
item.description: I’m not sure who first compared ChatGPT to Cliff Clavin, the garrulous mailman/barfly from TV’s Cheers. It’s so an apt comparison that many people probably came up with it independently. Cliff did know a lot of stuff—after all, he was a Jeopardy almost-winner.
item.dc:creator: Harry McCracken, Fast Company

item.title: From data to decisions: Graham Construction’s digital journey
item.description: When I joined Graham three years ago, I became the first person in my current position. While data and analytics were not entirely new to the company, there was no enterprise-wide approach. As a result, we embarked on this journey to create a cohesive enterprise data strategy.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Microsoft Has Nothing to Fear from Chinese GenAI Upstart DeepSeek
item.description: Microsoft's Dominance in AI Unchallenged by Cheap Chatbot
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Claves para que el CIO fiche al mejor equipo de TI
item.description: Uno de los sueños de cualquier dirigente, sea o no de TI, es, sin duda, formar un ‘dream team‘, donde cada persona aporte su valor, en el que poder confiar plenamente y que se entienda y trabaje al unísono. La realidad no siempre resulta tan positiva.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: NBN Co delivers free 600,000 fibre upgrades, with plenty more still to come – here's how to get yourself in the next batch
item.description: NBN Co has announced nearly 600,000 homes have taken advantage of its free fibre upgrade program with many more homes are already eligible. Find out if you're one of them and the best plans to sign-up for.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: OSINT gets its own subcommittee on House intelligence panel
item.description: The intelligence community has increasingly prioritized OSINT in recent years. Now, House lawmakers are creating a new subcommittee dedicated to the discipline. The post OSINT gets its own subcommittee on House intelligence panel first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Streaming is finally profitable. It offers a lesson in patience
item.description: Just a couple of years ago, pundits were warning of streaming’s demise. From Netflix to Spotify, these companies were burning through cash. How could they keep operating? Now, almost all of the streamers have made it to positive profits.
item.dc:creator: Henry Chandonnet, Fast Company

item.title: Broadcom’s Approach to Sovereign Cloud: Enabling Innovation and Control for Customers
item.description: In my many customer conversations at our recent European user conference, VMware Explore Barcelona, one message was clear: The strategic importance of data is fueling demand for sovereign cloud services.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Russian cybercrime network targeted for sanctions across US, UK and Australia
item.description: The U.S., U.K. and Australia have sanctioned a Russian web-hosting services provider and two Russian men who administer the service in support of Russian ransomware syndicate LockBit.
item.dc:creator: Fatima Hussein, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Scientists say several thousand earthquakes detected near Greece's island of Santorini
item.description: Scientists have detected several thousand earthquakes, the vast majority of them with small magnitudes, in just over two weeks near Greece’s volcanic island of Santorini.
item.dc:creator: Elena Becatoros, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Vance rips 'excessive' AI regulation at Paris summit
item.description: Vice President Vance slammed "excessive regulation" of artificial intelligence (AI) on Tuesday, making clear the Trump administration's stance on the emerging tech on an international stage.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Opinion: Tesla’s awful numbers put Musk back into campaign mode
item.description: Very little looks good on EV company's balance sheet, but Tesla chairman tries every way possible to spark excitement.
item.dc:creator: Liam Denning, The Mercury News

item.title: Esports Olympics to debut in Saudi Arabia later than expected in 2027
item.description: The International Olympic Committee says the first Olympic Esports Games will be hosted in Riyadh in 2027.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Ukraine's rare earth elements could help keep US military aid flowing
item.description: Ukraine has offered to strike a deal with U.S. President Donald Trump for continued American military aid in exchange for developing Ukraine’s mineral industry.
item.dc:creator: Samya Kullab, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Your ‘new’ Seagate data center hard drive is likely a used one
item.description: A growing number of buyers have reported purchasing supposedly new Seagate data center-grade hard drives, only to discover that they had been previously used for thousands of hours.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Trump's AI ambition and China's DeepSeek overshadow an AI summit in Paris
item.description: PARIS (AP) — The geopolitics of artificial intelligence will be in focus at a major summit in France where world leaders, executives and experts will hammer out pledges on guiding the development of the rapidly advancing technology.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET and KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: Bill would ban DeepSeek
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How the slash-and-burn tactics Musk brought to Washington backfired at Twitter
item.description: When Elon Musk bought Twitter in 2022, he laid off thousands of employees, stopped paying rent and auctioned off coffee makers and office chairs in hopes of a big turnaround.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN and BERNARD CONDON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Government Procurement Efficiency List: Reducing Regulatory Burden
item.description: Some regulations impose compliance or administrative costs/burdens that have no relation to the work to be performed under a government contract. The post Government Procurement Efficiency List: Reducing Regulatory Burden first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Roger Waldron, Federal News Network

item.title: Amazon to pay nearly $4M to settle lawsuit alleging it took tips from drivers
item.description: Amazon has agreed to pay nearly $4 million to settle charges that the e-commerce company subsidized its labor costs by taking tips its delivery drivers received from customers, District of Columbia Attorney General Brian L. Schwalb said Friday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: How AI could replace government workers to reduce spending
item.description: Elon Musk and DOGE are reportedly using artificial intelligence to analyze federal spending and where cuts an be made
item.dc:creator: Robert Sherman, NewsNation

item.title: AWS plans to outspend Microsoft and Google on AI infrastructure
item.description: Amazon Web Services (AWS) intends to invest $100 billion in ramping up infrastructure for AI cloud services this year, surpassing the spending plans of rivals Microsoft and Google.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Network jobs watch: Hiring, skills and certification trends
item.description: Network and infrastructure roles continue to shift as enterprises adopt technologies such as AI-driven network operations, multicloud networking, zero trust network access (ZTNA), and SD-WAN.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: The hidden threat of neglected cloud infrastructure
item.description: In 2012, the Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) issued a public advisory regarding software to monitor and control building systems.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: New law in Brazil is making students put away their smartphones at school
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — Brazilian students returned to class this week with a new task: staying away from their smartphones as a new law restricting their use in schools took effect.
item.dc:creator: GABRIELA SÁ PESSOA, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: OpenAI looks across US for sites to build its Trump-backed Stargate AI data centers
item.description: OpenAI is scouring the U.S. for sites to build a network of huge data centers to power its artificial intelligence technology, expanding beyond a flagship Texas location and looking across 16 states to accelerate the Stargate project championed by President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Digging into voice AI platform Deepgram
item.description: Deepgram is the leading voice AI platform used by over 200,000 developers to build speech-to-text, text-to-speech, and full speech-to-speech (which enables individuals with speech disabilities to be clearly understood) tools.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: One Tech Tip: How to block your phone from tracking your location
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Smartphones are useful tools for everyday life, but they're privy to nearly everything about you, including all the places you've been — if you let them.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Google scraps its diversity hiring goals as it complies with Trump's new government contractor rules
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Google is scrapping some of its diversity hiring targets, joining a lengthening list of U.S. companies that have abandoned or scaled back their diversity, equity and inclusion programs.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Fast fashion, laptops and toys are likely to cost more due to US tariffs on Chinese imports
item.description: A sweeping new U.S. tariff on products made in China is expected to increase the prices American consumers pay for a wide array of products, from the ultra-cheap apparel sold on online shopping platforms to toys and electronic devices such as computers and cellphones.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News
